name: Gerri de la Sal and Lake Montcortès Loop
name_original: Lago de Moncortés desde Gerri de la Sal — circular por el Pallars Sobirá
description: This difficult 28.5-mile mountain biking route from Gerri de la Sal explores the Pallars Sobirá, featuring Lake Montcortés and historic site
region_intro: Mountain biking around Baix Pallars offers diverse terrain nestled between the Pyrenees and Pre-Pyrenees, traversed by the Noguera Pallaresa river. The region features wild mountains, deep ravines, and areas along the river, providing varied challenges for riders. Trails incorporate rock, dirt, roots, and gravel, with options ranging from steep climbs to twisty descents. This area is part of the UNESCO-recognized Geoparc Orígens, highlighting its geological and natural heritage. Best mountain bike trails around Baix Pallars The most popular mountain bike trail is Gerri de la Sal and Lake Montcortès Loop, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around the natural Montcortès Lake. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Pantano de San Antonio – La Coma Trail loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses, a moderate 33.2 miles (53.4 km) path. This route offers views of Pantano de San Antonio and varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Gerri de la Sal – Refugio de Cuberes loop from Baro, a 43.0 miles (69.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Baix Pallars is defined by wild mountains, the Noguera Pallaresa river, and long natural descents. The network offers options for different ability levels, with more than 60 routes available. The routes in Baix Pallars are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Baix Pallars's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Baix Pallars?
A: Baix Pallars offers a comprehensive network of over 60 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 39 difficult routes to explore the diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Baix Pallars?
A: The terrain in Baix Pallars is highly varied, reflecting its position between the Pyrenees and Pre-Pyrenees. Riders can expect a mix of rock, dirt, roots, and gravel. Trails often feature steep sections and twisty descents, with long natural descents being a hallmark of the region, transitioning from rocky alpine areas to forest soils.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike routes in Baix Pallars?
A: Yes, Baix Pallars has 6 easy mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails offer a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Baix Pallars?
A: Many trails offer stunning natural sights. You can encounter impressive features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegats Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dramatic scenery, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Baix Pallars?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Baix Pallars are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gerri de la Sal – Refugio de Cuberes loop from Baro&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 69.2 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Baix Pallars?
A: The mountain biking experience in Baix Pallars is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Riders frequently praise the wild and authentic nature of the trails, the long natural descents, and the diverse landscapes that offer a layered riding experience.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Noguera Pallaresa river?
A: Yes, the Noguera Pallaresa river is a central feature of the region, and many trails run alongside or offer views of it. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17548710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gerri de la Sal – Kayak and Canoe Slalom Course loop from Gerri de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; explores areas near the river.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Mur Church – Gerri de la Sal loop from Gerri de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; allows you to combine your ride with a visit to historical sites like the Santa María de Mur Church and the town of Gerri de la Sal, known for its historical saltworks.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Baix Pallars?
A: The diverse terrain means conditions can vary. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for mountain biking. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher mountain areas, limiting access to some trails.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, Baix Pallars features 18 moderate mountain bike routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pantano de San Antonio – La Coma Trail loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses&lt;/a&gt;, a 53.4 km path offering varied terrain and views of Pantano de San Antonio.

Q: What are some of the geological highlights I might encounter while mountain biking?
A: Baix Pallars is part of the UNESCO-recognized Geoparc Orígens, highlighting its significant geological heritage. You'll ride through landscapes shaped by ancient geological processes, including deep ravines and rock formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3231895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Serpent&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a geological feature you might find nearby.
tips: Lake and one of the most important waterfowl reserves in the Pyrenees

Beautiful gothic style bridge.
The bridge that crosses the Noguera Pallaresa river is a must if you pass through Gerri de la Sal.

Romanesque church, which was part of the old monastery (of which nothing remains). The church was founded in 1149, and the bell tower is from the 13th century. I recommend booking a guided tour.
Located in a very quiet area next to the river, where we can rest or cool off.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Lago de Moncortés desde Gerri de la Sal mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging mountain bike route, classified as hard. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Expect significant elevation gains and technical sections that may require you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain in the Pallars Sobirá region is diverse and wild. You will encounter a variety of surfaces, including rock, dirt, roots, and gravel. The trails are often raw and natural, requiring focus and offering an exhilarating experience amidst high mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular natural landscapes and cultural landmarks. A significant highlight is Lake Montcortés, an important waterfowl reserve. You'll also pass by the beautiful Gothic Monastery Bridge of Gerri de la Sal and the Romanesque Santa María de Mur Church, providing a rich historical context.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this mountain bike tour from Gerri de la Sal?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Gerri de la Sal is the starting point, and you should look for designated parking within or near the village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Lago de Moncortés desde Gerri de la Sal trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in the Pyrenees. However, given the challenging nature of this mountain bike route and the presence of a waterfowl reserve at Lake Montcortés, it's best to keep dogs on a leash, especially near wildlife, and ensure they are fit enough for a long, strenuous ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October), when the weather is more stable and the high mountain passes are clear of snow. Always check local weather conditions before you go, as the Pyrenees can experience rapid changes.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required to ride this mountain bike trail. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas like the Lake Montcortés waterfowl reserve.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of official trails such as Etapa 14, El Camí IT1TR6 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer, and Estany de Montcortès i Bosc Encantat, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging mountain bike ride like this?
faqs.answer: Given the difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves), and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or get refreshments in Gerri de la Sal after the ride?
faqs.answer: Gerri de la Sal is a small village and offers some basic amenities. You can typically find local restaurants or cafes to refuel and relax after completing your challenging mountain bike adventure.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to Gerri de la Sal?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Gerri de la Sal are limited. It's generally more convenient to reach the starting point by car. If relying on public transport, research bus schedules well in advance, as services might be infrequent, especially outside of peak tourist season.

waypoints.name: Monastery Bridge of Gerri de la Sal
